# identity — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02
# Ticket: ARCHITECT-IDENTITY-0001

## Feature Overview

The identity feature is the platform bootstrap and actor-directory refresh layer for VCSM. It is a minimal-surface, infrastructure-tier feature with no owned UI — its job is to provision platform identity rows for a new or returning user (via a SECURITY DEFINER RPC), refresh the actor directory after VC source-of-truth mutations, and wire the shared identity engine to the VCSM-specific app context resolver at startup. The companion state layer at `apps/VCSM/src/state/identity/` manages the in-memory `IdentityProvider`, actor switching, self-heal, and context resolution — it is co-owned but architecturally separate.

**Source Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/`
**Engine Path:** `engines/identity/src/` (full identity engine — VCSM consumes via `@identity` alias and injects resolver via `configureIdentityEngine()`)
**State Layer Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/state/identity/` (IdentityProvider, actor switching, self-heal — companion, not subpath)

---

| Layer | Present | Path |

| Controllers | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/controller/ |
| DALs | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/dal/ |
| Models | NO | None — RPC responses consumed raw (identified gap) |
| Hooks | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/hooks/ |
| Screens | NO | No UI surface — feature is bootstrap-only |
| Components | NO | No components owned by this feature |

| Resolvers (non-standard) | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/resolvers/ |
| Setup (non-standard) | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/setup.js |
| Engine controllers | YES | engines/identity/src/controller/ (3 controllers) |
| Engine DALs | YES | engines/identity/src/dal/ (11 DAL files) |
| Engine models | YES | engines/identity/src/model/ (6 model files) |
| Engine services | YES | engines/identity/src/services/ (7 service files) |

---

## Active Controllers

### Feature Layer — apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/controller/

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| ensureVcsmPlatformBootstrap.controller.js | Provisions 6 platform identity objects via SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Idempotent — safe to call on every login. | Requires `userId` + `actorId` params; DB-level: VF-01 OPEN — no `auth.uid()` guard in live function |
| refreshActorDirectory.controller.js | Hollow pass-through — re-exports `refreshVcActorDirectory` and `refreshActorDirectoryRow` from the DAL with no added logic | No auth gate — hollow pass-through (IRONMAN RISK-5 open item) |

### Engine Layer — engines/identity/src/controller/

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| resolveAuthenticatedContext.controller.js | Full 8-step identity resolution: session → app → access → account → state/prefs → actors → active actor → context. Includes result cache (TTL 120s). | Session required (step 1); access gate (step 3) — throws `ACCESS_DENIED` if not granted |
| switchActiveActor.controller.js | Updates active actor in preferences and state. | Session required; actor link ownership enforced |
| logoutCleanup.controller.js | Clears in-memory caches and fires logout events. | Session token cleared by Supabase auth |

**Note:** `refreshActorDirectory.controller.js` is a hollow pass-through with no business logic. It is an open IRONMAN decision item (RISK-5) — either promote to a true controller or remove and let consumers call the adapter/DAL directly.

---

## Active DALs

### Feature Layer — apps/VCSM/src/features/identity/dal/

| DAL | Tables / RPCs | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| provision.rpc.dal.js | `platform.provision_vcsm_identity(p_user_id, p_actor_id)` RPC — provisions: `user_app_access`, `user_app_accounts`, `user_app_preferences`, `user_app_state`, `user_app_actor_links`, `vc.actors.user_app_account_id` | SECURITY DEFINER RPC. VF-01 OPEN: no `auth.uid()` guard in live body. Return type is `jsonb` (comments incorrectly say `uuid` — VLF-01). Migration `20260518040000` ready, deployment UNKNOWN. |
| refreshActorDirectory.dal.js | `identity.refresh_actor_directory_row(p_actor_domain, p_actor_id)` RPC | No tracked creation migration. `refreshVcActorDirectory()` is a convenience wrapper for `refreshActorDirectoryRow('vc', actorId)`. |

### Engine DAL Layer — engines/identity/src/dal/

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| session.read.dal.js | `supabase.auth.*` wrappers | Auth session resolution |
| app.read.dal.js | `platform.apps` | App row by key |
| access.read.dal.js | `platform.user_app_access` | Access grant check |
| account.read.dal.js | `platform.user_app_accounts`, `platform.v_user_app_context` | Account resolution |
| actorLinks.read.dal.js | `platform.user_app_actor_links` | Read actor links |
| actorLinks.write.dal.js | `platform.user_app_actor_links`, `platform.user_app_preferences` | Write actor switch |
| roles.read.dal.js | `platform.app_roles`, `platform.user_app_account_roles`, `platform.role_capabilities` | Role reads |
| capabilities.read.dal.js | `platform.capabilities`, `platform.user_capabilities` | Capability reads |
| preferences.read.dal.js | `platform.user_app_preferences` | Preferences read |
| state.read.dal.js | `platform.user_app_state` | State read |
| state.write.dal.js | `platform.user_app_state` | Login timestamp, destination write |

## Active Hooks

| Hook | What It Calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| useIdentityOps.js | `refreshVcActorDirectory` (via controller), `ensureVcsmPlatformBootstrap` (via controller) | Exposes identity operation functions to React layer without maintaining component state |

**Note:** The `useIdentity()` hook and `IdentityProvider` are NOT owned by this feature — they live in `apps/VCSM/src/state/identity/identityContext.jsx` and are re-exported via the feature adapter for convenience.

---

## Non-Standard Layers

| Layer | File | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Resolvers | resolvers/vcsmIdentity.resolver.js | Injectable app context resolver — queries `platform.user_app_actor_links` for VCSM; injected into engine via `configureIdentityEngine()`. Returns `actorLinks`, `roleKeys`, `capabilityKeys`, `isSuspended`, `defaultDestination`. | IRONMAN RISK-9: non-standard layer — taxonomy decision pending |
| Setup | setup.js | One-time DI wiring at app startup — calls `configureIdentityEngine()` with supabase client + VCSM resolver. Guarded by `_configured` flag (idempotent). | Non-standard; called once from `main.jsx` |

---

## Adapter Exports

### identity.adapter.js (primary public surface)
- `useIdentityOps` — from hooks
- `ensureVcsmPlatformBootstrap`, `refreshVcActorDirectory` — from identityOps.adapter
- `useIdentity`, `IdentityProvider` — from `@/state/identity/identityContext`

### identityOps.adapter.js (internal ops surface)
- `ensureVcsmPlatformBootstrap` — from controller
- `refreshVcActorDirectory` — from controller

---

## Engine Dependencies

| Engine | Import Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| identity | `@identity` (alias → `engines/identity/index.js`) | `configureIdentityEngine` — DI entry point used in `setup.js`; full engine exports available via adapters |

The engine is configured once at startup via `setupVcsmIdentityEngine()`. The engine then drives the full 8-step authentication resolution path (`resolveAuthenticatedContext`), actor switching (`switchActiveActor`), and logout cleanup (`logoutCleanup`). The state layer at `state/identity/` consumes these engine exports.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Consumer Feature | What Is Imported | Direction | Via |
|---|---|---|---|
| settings (privacy, profile, account, vports) | `useIdentity`, `useIdentityOps` | identity → settings (consumed) | identity.adapter.js |
| post (postcard, screens) | `useIdentity` | identity → post (consumed) | state/identity/identityContext or identity.adapter.js |
| chat (inbox screens) | `useIdentity` or identity adapter | identity → chat (consumed) | identity.adapter.js |
| professional/briefings | `useIdentity` | identity → professional (consumed) | identity.adapter.js |
| ads | `useIdentity` | identity → ads (consumed) | state/identity/identityContext (direct — bypasses adapter) |
| app/providers | `IdentityProvider` | identity → app bootstrap | identity.adapter.js |
| app/layout, app/routes | `useIdentity` | identity → layout/routing | state/identity/identityContext (direct) |

**Boundary risk:** 105 cross-feature import sites. Some consumers import from `@/state/identity/identityContext` directly rather than `@/features/identity/adapters/identity.adapter`. This bypasses the adapter boundary. The two surfaces differ — `identityContext` exports `useIdentityDisplayDeprecated` which is not on the adapter. Mixed import paths exist across features.

---

## Authorization Pattern

The identity feature itself has no ownership-scoped authorization gate — it is the gate. The provisioning controller (`ensureVcsmPlatformBootstrap`) only validates that `userId` and `actorId` are non-null before delegating to the RPC. The RPC is where authorization should live. The critical open finding (VF-01) is that the live RPC `platform.provision_vcsm_identity` has NO `auth.uid()` guard — any authenticated user can provision platform rows for any `userId` passed as a parameter. Migration `20260518040000` patches this but deployment status is UNKNOWN.

The engine-level `resolveAuthenticatedContext` enforces a session check (step 1) and an access grant check (step 3) before any actor or account resolution proceeds.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**DEPENDENT**

The identity feature is the platform foundation — 37+ source files across the app import from it. It owns no UI but is consumed by virtually every feature that needs actor context. It has a mandatory dependency on `engines/identity` for the full resolution pipeline, and the companion `state/identity/` layer is tightly coupled to the engine's exported controllers and events. The feature cannot function in isolation.

---

## Architecture State

**EVOLVING**

The core provisioning and directory refresh paths are well-structured (RPC-based, idempotent, guarded). The non-standard `resolvers/` and `setup.js` layers are undocumented and pending taxonomy decisions (IRONMAN RISK-3, RISK-9). The `refreshActorDirectory.controller.js` is a hollow pass-through with no business logic — a structural inconsistency. The model layer is absent (RPC responses consumed raw). Mixed adapter/direct import patterns across 105 consumer sites create boundary drift. Two open security findings (VF-01, VF-02) in the live DB RPC keep this feature from STABLE.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **VF-01 CRITICAL (OPEN):** `platform.provision_vcsm_identity` has NO `auth.uid()` guard in live DB. Any authenticated user can provision platform identity rows for any `userId`. Migration `20260518040000` is written but deployment status is UNKNOWN. If undeployed, cross-user identity poisoning is live on production.
2. **VF-02 MEDIUM (OPEN):** `pg_temp` missing from `search_path` in live function. Covered by same migration `20260518040000`.
3. **VLF-02 MEDIUM (OPEN):** `p_actor_id` is `DEFAULT NULL` in live RPC body — provisioning without actor link is possible. Intended behavior is unclear.
4. **VLF-01 LOW (OPEN):** DAL comments state return type is `uuid`; live function returns `jsonb`. Comment mismatch creates future maintenance risk.
5. **VLF-03 LOW (OPEN):** `service_role` lacks EXECUTE grant on `provision_vcsm_identity`. Only `authenticated` role has EXECUTE.
6. **Hollow controller:** `refreshActorDirectory.controller.js` re-exports two DAL functions with no added logic. Not a true controller layer. IRONMAN RISK-5 — decision pending.
7. **No model layer:** RPC responses are consumed raw. No model file exists to transform provisioning results into a domain object. Identified as a gap in IRONMAN 2026-05-18.
8. **Mixed import boundaries:** ~105 consumer sites; some import from `@/state/identity/identityContext` directly rather than through the adapter. `useIdentityDisplayDeprecated` on the direct path is not on the adapter surface.
9. **No tracked creation migrations:** Neither `platform.provision_vcsm_identity` nor `identity.refresh_actor_directory_row` has a tracked creation migration in `apps/VCSM/supabase/migrations/`. CARNAGE open item.
10. **Non-standard `resolvers/` layer:** `vcsmIdentity.resolver.js` is an undocumented non-standard layer inside the feature — IRONMAN RISK-9 taxonomy decision required.
11. **Zero test coverage:** SPIDER-MAN has never run. No test files found. Critical-tier feature with no regression safety net.

---

| Engine dependencies mapped | PASS | @identity alias mapped; configureIdentityEngine DI documented | None missing |
| Tests/validation noted | FAIL | SPIDER-MAN never run; zero test files | No regression coverage for CRITICAL-tier feature |

## Recommended Handoffs

- **DB** — Confirm deployment status of migration `20260518040000_platform_provision_vcsm_identity.sql`. If not deployed, escalate to P0. If deployed, close VF-01 and VF-02.
- **VENOM** — Re-run after migration status confirmed. Verify `auth.uid()` guard is live. Audit `vcsmIdentity.resolver.js` for injection attack surface.
- **SPIDER-MAN** — Run first test coverage audit on this CRITICAL-tier feature. Bootstrap path and provisioning RPC are the priority targets.
- **SENTRY** — Architecture contract compliance check — verify adapter boundary is being respected across 105 consumer sites; confirm `resolvers/` layer classification.
- **IRONMAN** — Close open decisions: RISK-5 (hollow controller) and RISK-9 (resolvers/ taxonomy). Assign named feature owner.

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

The feature has a clear purpose, working DALs, adapters, a hook, and engine integration. The missing pieces are: model layer (gap), named owner (gap), hollow controller (structural inconsistency), zero test coverage (CRITICAL risk), and an unresolved DB-level security finding (VF-01) that is the highest-priority open item in the platform.
